candidate_problems.text: Test whether measures of 'epistemic infrastructure' such as reporting fidelity, cross-agency learning, and trust predict long-run safety gains better than spending, staffing, or demographic variables in matched cities.
Determine which feedback loops are most leverage-bearing: witness cooperation, hospital-police data integration, violence interruption networks, or administrative memory across political turnover.

Examine cities that became safer despite weak legitimacy or fragmented institutions to see whether the knowledge-processing account is incomplete or whether alternative informational channels compensated.
